sales.title:
SCYTHIA. Geto-Dacians. Coson (ca. after 54 BC). AV stater (21mm, 8.37 gm, 12h). NGC Gem MS 5/5 - 5/5. Sarmatia, Olbia. KOΣΩN, Roman consul (L. Junius Brutus) walking left, accompanied by two lictors fore and aft, ΟΛΒ monogram in left field / Eagle standing left on scepter, with spread wings, clutching laurel wreath in right talons; pellet below raised foot. RPC I 1701a. Stunning, buttery toning on well struck dies worthy of its grade. Ex Freeman & Sear, Mail Bid Sale 12 (28 October 2005), lot 96. HID05401242017 © 2025 Heritage Auctions | All Rights Reserved www.HA.com/TexasAuctioneerLicenseNotice

sales.title: Anonymous. Ca. 218-216 BC. AV stater (18mm, 6.84 gm, 5h). NGC Choice XF 5/5 - 2/5, Fine Style, scuffs. Rome. Laureate, janiform head of Dioscuri; dotted border / Oath-taking scene of youth kneeling left, head reverted, holding pig, two soldiers standing facing him on either side, each with grounded spear in left hand and touching pig with sword in right hand; ROMA in exergue. Crawford 28/1 (225-212 BC). Sydenham 69.  Ex Classical Numismatic Group, Auction 103 (14 September 2016), lot 611; Lawrence A. Adams Collection (Classical Numismatic Group, Triton XIX, 5 January 2016), lot 2179; UBS, Auction 45 (15 September 1998), lot 370; J. Schulman, (29 September 1919), private sale with old dealer's tag included.   This issue, the first gold coinage of the Roman Republic, was minted at a time of national crisis. The Second Punic War (218–201 BC) was a pivotal conflict between the Roman Republic and Carthage that reshaped the balance of power in the ancient Mediterranean. Sparked by growing tensions after the First Punic War and Hannibal Barca’s bold attack on the Spanish city of Saguntum, the war is best remembered for Hannibal’s daring Alpine crossing and his early victories at Trebia, Lake Trasimene, and Cannae—battles that brought Rome to the brink of collapse. Despite these devastating losses, Rome refused to surrender, displaying their resolve with this powerful reverse type.   HID05401242017  © 2025 Heritage Auctions | All Rights Reserved www.HA.com/TexasAuctioneerLicenseNotice

sales.title:
SKYTHIA. Geto-Dacians. Koson, ca. mid-1st Century B.C. AV Stater (8.38 gms). NGC MS, Strike: 5/5 Surface: 2/5. Bent.
RPC-1, 1701A; HGC-3.2, 2049. Obverse: Roman consul accompanied by two lictors advancing left; monogram to left; Reverse: Eagle, holding wreath, standing left on scepter. Despite the flan being slightly bent, this specimen otherwise glistens with an abundance of brilliance commensurate with its Mint State level of preservation.
